title: Muon Tomography (cosmic-ray scattering imaging)
sub_domain: High-Z imaging via cosmic-ray muon MCS
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence boilerplate. Physics grounded in Borozdin et al. 2003 Nature demo; Morris et al. 2008 tomographic imaging of waste casks; Fujii et al. 2020 Fukushima reactor imaging; Tanaka 2022 review.

E.description: Atmospheric cosmic-ray muons (GeV energies, flux ~1/cm^2/min at sea level) traverse samples; multiple-Coulomb-scattering angle sigma_theta = (13.6/p) * sqrt(L/X_0) encodes radiation length X_0, which varies as ~1/Z^2. Tracker detectors above/below the sample record incoming/outgoing trajectories; voxelized reconstruction of X_0 via PoCA or EM.
E.world_state_x: 3D radiation-length map X_0(r) (or equivalent Z-effective map)
E.observation_y: Tracker hits, scattering angles, path lengths per muon event
physical_parameters_theta: Cosmic-ray flux density and energy spectrum

W.uniqueness: Z_eff map approx unique given uniform cosmic-ray angular distribution
W.regime: Reconstruction is Poisson-limited at low flux; angular resolution O(10 mrad) at GeV. Hybrid stopping-power techniques (muon decay counting) add info.

C.solver_class: PoCA (Point of Closest Approach), EM / MLEM over voxels, MART, learned (muon-CNN)
C.error_bound: Z_eff uncertainty ~ 1/sqrt(N_muon * voxel_solid_angle); resolution ~ detector_res + MCS_spread
C.complexity: PoCA O(N_muon); MLEM O(iter * N_muon * N_voxel)
